summary: Small game-mode decomp matching PR touching gm_1601, gm_16AE, gm_16F1, and gmmain_lib. It tightened function prototypes, changed a few expression shapes, introduced a static inline spawn-point helper, removed likely artificial small-data labels, and synchronized headers. The decomp.dev bot reported matched code rising to 66.46% (+0.02%, +668 bytes), with new 100% matches for gm_16AE fn_8016DEEC and gm_1601 fn_8016989C; it also reported one regression in fn_801695BC.
key_files: src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c - changed fn_801695BC and fn_8016989C argument widths from s32-style to u8 and removed an explicit s32 cast at a callsite
src/melee/gm/gm_1601.h - synchronized prototypes for fn_801695BC and fn_8016989C
src/melee/gm/gm_16AE.c - changed pre-increment to post-increment in fn_8016D634 and introduced static inline getSpawnPoint used by fn_8016DEEC, fn_8016E2BC, and gm_8016EDDC
src/melee/gm/gm_16F1.c - changed fn_8016FAD4 kind parameter from u32 to int, zero-initialized rankings[7], rewrote gm_80173EEC_inline skip logic as early continues
src/melee/gm/gm_16F1.static.h - removed static lbl_804DA2E8, lbl_804DA2EC, and lbl_804DA2EE declarations
src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.c - changed gmMainLib_8015DADC return type from s32 to bool
what_changed: fn_8016989C now takes u8 arg1 and u8 arg2 instead of wider integer types; the caller in gm_801674C4 no longer casts Player_GetCostumeId(0) to s32.
fn_801695BC now takes u8 arg1 and u8 arg2 instead of s32 arg1 and s32 arg2; its header declaration was updated the same way.
fn_8016D634 changed the counter expression from ++lbl_8046B6A0.unk_30 to lbl_8046B6A0.unk_30++ in the threshold check against lbl_8046B6A0.x24C8.xD.

gm_16AE.c gained static inline getSpawnPoint(int i, Vec3* v), which resolves FighterMatchInfo[i].spawn_point == -1 to the player index and otherwise passes the configured spawn_point into Stage_80224E64.
Repeated inline spawn-point selection blocks in fn_8016DEEC, fn_8016E2BC, and gm_8016EDDC were replaced with getSpawnPoint calls.
fn_8016FAD4 changed its kind parameter from u32 to int and replaced manual byte copying from lbl_804DA2E8/lbl_804DA2EC/lbl_804DA2EE into rankings with u8 rankings[7] = { 0 }.
gm_16F1.static.h removed the static small-data declarations lbl_804DA2E8, lbl_804DA2EC, and lbl_804DA2EE after fn_8016FAD4 stopped using them.
gm_80173EEC_inline rewrote one large compound condition into a series of early continue checks for constants 0x29, 0x42, 0x43, 0xB9, 0xC9, 0xCA, and 9, followed by if (!gmMainLib_8015DADC(i)).
gmMainLib_8015DADC was retyped from s32 to bool in both implementation and header.
smart_moves: Using a static inline getSpawnPoint avoided repeated hand-written spawn-point branches while preserving inlining; the bot reported fn_8016DEEC reaching 100% and improvements in fn_8016E2BC and gm_8016EDDC.
Narrowing character/costume-like parameters to u8 matched callsite data better and removed an explicit cast; this coincided with fn_8016989C reaching 100%.
Changing a predicate-like bitset query, gmMainLib_8015DADC, to bool made callers express the check as !gmMainLib_8015DADC(i), which fits the rewritten gm_80173EEC_inline control flow.
Replacing synthetic-looking static data loads with rankings[7] = { 0 } both simplified fn_8016FAD4 and removed three static labels; the bot reported .sdata2 improving from 50.00% to 80.00%.
The PR used very small source-shape changes, such as pre-increment versus post-increment and early continues, to move stubborn near-matches without broad semantic rewrites.
decomp_lessons: For gm functions that pass player character, costume, or similar byte-sized IDs, test u8 prototypes before keeping s32 parameters; width changes can affect casts, sign/zero extension, and matching.
When the same branch pattern appears in several neighboring functions, a static inline helper can be a matching tool, not just a cleanup refactor.
Spawn-point logic in gm_16AE follows the pattern: if FighterMatchInfo[i].spawn_point == -1, call Stage_80224E64 with i; otherwise call it with the stored spawn_point.

Do not assume unmatched .sdata2 labels are real constants; in this PR, replacing manual loads from lbl_804DA2E8/lbl_804DA2EC/lbl_804DA2EE with a zero initializer improved matching and allowed the labels to be removed.
Large compound conditions with unsigned range checks may need to be expressed as ordered early exits or continues to match compiler branch layout.
Header prototypes must be updated immediately with C source type discoveries; this PR kept gm_1601.h, gm_16F1.h, and gmmain_lib.h in sync with implementation changes.
naming_conventions: The PR preserved existing address-based placeholder names such as fn_8016DEEC, fn_8016FAD4, gm_8016EDDC, and lbl_8046B6A0.
The only new semantic helper name in the diff is lowerCamelCase getSpawnPoint.
Struct/member names already present in the code, such as FighterMatchInfo, spawn_point, PlayerInitData, and Vec3, were reused rather than renamed.
Public header declarations retain address comments like /* 1695BC */ and /* 16FAD4 */ while updating only the types.
No human naming review was present in the slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Try post-increment instead of pre-increment when a counter compare is nearly matching; fn_8016D634 changed ++unk_30 to unk_30++.
Use static inline helpers to force repeated code to inline with a shared source shape; getSpawnPoint was introduced for Stage_80224E64 spawn resolution.
Adjust parameter signedness and width to match actual ABI/codegen behavior: s32 to u8 for fn_801695BC/fn_8016989C arguments and u32 to int for fn_8016FAD4 kind.
Prefer bool return types for predicate/bitset queries when callers branch on truthiness; gmMainLib_8015DADC was changed from s32 to bool.
Use u8 rankings[7] = { 0 } when zero-initialized stack bytes match better than manual copies from small-data symbols.
Rewrite dense conditions as sequential continue statements to shape branch order and avoid complex combined comparisons.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code: 66.46% (+0.02%, +668 bytes).
decomp.dev bot reported 2 new matches: main/melee/gm/gm_16AE fn_8016DEEC (+19 bytes, 96.82% to 100.00%) and main/melee/gm/gm_1601 fn_8016989C (+1 byte, 99.80% to 100.00%).
decomp.dev bot reported improvements in fn_8016E2BC, fn_8016FAD4, gm_16F1 .sdata2, gm_8016EDDC, gm_8016A22C, and fn_8016D634.
decomp.dev bot reported one regression: main/melee/gm/gm_1601 fn_801695BC (-4 bytes, 70.85% to 70.22%).
follow_up_queries: Revisit fn_801695BC because the u8 argument change caused a bot-reported -4 byte regression.
Search other gm_16AE spawn-point callsites for the getSpawnPoint pattern: FighterMatchInfo[i].spawn_point == -1 ? i : spawn_point.
Audit remaining gmMainLib bitset/predicate helpers for s32 return types that should be bool.
Investigate nearby gm_16F1 small-data symbols, including lbl_804DA2F0/lbl_804DA2F4/lbl_804DA2F6, to distinguish real constants from zero-initializer artifacts.
Check whether gm_80173EEC_inline has further match potential after the early-continue rewrite, since the bot report did not list it as a new match.
